name: Mercury
description: The smallest planet and closest to the Sun. Discovered in ancient times. Type: Terrestrial. Unlikely to support life due to extreme temperatures.

name: Venus
description: The hottest planet with a thick toxic atmosphere. Discovered in ancient times. Type: Terrestrial. Cannot support known life forms.

name: Earth
description: Our home planet with liquid water and breathable air. Discovered: Known to ancients. Type: Terrestrial. Highly suitable for supporting diverse life.

name: Mars
description: The red planet with polar ice caps and evidence of past water. Discovered in ancient times. Type: Terrestrial. Moderate potential for microbial life.

name: Jupiter
description: The largest planet with a Great Red Spot storm. Discovered in ancient times. Type: Gas Giant. Cannot support life as we know it.

name: Saturn
description: Famous for its beautiful ring system. Discovered in ancient times. Type: Gas Giant. Cannot support life on its surface.

name: Uranus
description: An ice giant that rotates on its side. Discovered: 1781. Type: Ice Giant. Cannot support life in its harsh environment.

name: Neptune
description: The windiest planet with deep blue color. Discovered: 1846. Type: Ice Giant. Cannot support life due to extreme pressure and cold.

name: Pluto
description: A distant dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt with a bright icy surface and a complex system of moons. Type: Dwarf Planet. Not likely to support life as we know it.

name: Ceres
description: The largest object in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter. Type: Dwarf Planet. Icy and rocky with a mysterious surface.

name: Haumea
description: A fast-spinning dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt with an elongated shape and a bright icy surface. Type: Dwarf Planet. Extremely cold and unlikely to support life.

name: Makemake
description: A distant dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt known for its reddish color and sparse surface. Type: Dwarf Planet. Too cold for life as we know it.

name: Eris
description: One of the most distant dwarf planets in the scattered disc with a highly reflective surface. Type: Dwarf Planet. Very cold and isolated from the Sun.
